original_abstract: In 1972, Erich Clar introduced his aromatic $\pi$-sextet rule, providing a robust qualitative framework for discussing the aromatic character of benzenoid species. Four decades later, Clar’s rule remains a steady source of inspiration for the chemical community, having been extensively validated by both experimental observations and theoretical models. In this review, we examine key examples that highlight the enduring utility of the $\pi$-sextet rule across diverse chemical environments. We conclude by discussing two recent, high-profile applications that demonstrate the rule’s continued relevance in contemporary research.
humanized_abstract: Erich Clar published his aromatic $\pi$-sextet rule in 1972, giving a very useful qualitative tool for deciding the degree of aromaticity of different types of benzenoid compounds. It has endured for the past 40 years and still remains a focal point for interest within the chemical community having been supported by vast amounts of experimental and computational evidence. In this review we provide an overview of the number of examples, which continually demonstrate the relevance of the $\pi$-sextet rule to aromaticity. Lastly, a couple of more recent examples of their use in more current research is discussed.

It has endured for the past 40 years and still remains a focal point for interest within the chemical community having been supported by vast amounts of experimental and computational evidence. In this review we provide an overview of the number of examples, which continually demonstrate the relevance of the $\pi$-sextet rule to aromaticity. Lastly, a couple of more recent examples of their use in more current research is discussed.
